evals.prompt: Review the skill in ./my-skills/deploy-staging for compliance.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs lint-skill.py on the folder, reports findings by severity scaled to the skill's phase, and — if there are no errors — reports that it passes at its phase, noting any warnings/infos needed to reach publish. It does not invent canonical tokens.
expectations: The skill runs the linter against the given folder (e.g. python3 scripts/lint-skill.py ./my-skills/deploy-staging).
Findings are reported with severities (error / warn / info) scaled to the skill's declared phase.
If there are no errors, the skill states the skill passes at its current phase and lists what is needed to advance.
The skill does not fabricate a canonical token to silence a finding.

evals.prompt: Check this skill -- its SKILL.md frontmatter has `description: TODO: fill in` and the folder is named util but the frontmatter says name: utils.
evals.expected_output: The linter flags the unquoted-colon frontmatter as invalid YAML and flags name != folder (utils vs util). The review explains both and proposes fixes (quote the description; make name equal the folder, or rename the folder), reported as errors that block the skill.
expectations: The review reports that the frontmatter is invalid YAML because of the unquoted 'description: TODO: ...' colon.
The review reports that name ('utils') does not match the folder ('util').
Both are reported as errors (blocking), with concrete fixes proposed.

evals.prompt: Is the skill at ./skills/release-notes ready to publish?
evals.expected_output: The skill lints against the publish bar (overriding or assuming publish phase as appropriate). Missing publish-bar items -- evals, license, no plaintext secrets, both doors if it has scripts -- are reported as blocking errors. It gives a clear ready / not-ready verdict with the specific blockers.
expectations: The review evaluates the skill against the publish bar (evals present, license set, no plaintext secrets, two doors if scripts exist).
Missing publish-bar items are reported as blocking for publish.
The review gives a clear ready / not-ready verdict listing the specific blockers.

evals.prompt: Lint every skill in ./skills and tell me which ones have problems.
evals.expected_output: The linter is run against the collection directory (every */SKILL.md under it), and the review summarizes per-skill findings, distinguishing skills with errors from those with only warnings/infos. It offers to fix them.
expectations: The linter is run over the whole collection (each */SKILL.md), not just one skill.
Results are summarized per skill, distinguishing skills with errors from those with only warnings/infos.
The review offers to apply fixes.
